encounter.reason: Diabetic retinopathy with macular edema evaluation and intravitreal treatment

participants.name: Dr. Neha Kapur, MD
participants.role: retina specialist, evaluating clinician and proceduralist

participants.name: Isaac Feldman, OD
participants.role: ophthalmic imaging, OCT, fundus examination findings

encounter.location: Sandia Retina Specialists, Albuquerque, New Mexico

labs.name: glucose
labs.note: Morning measurement reported by patient; specimen type not stated.

screening.name: visual acuity with correction, right eye
visual acuity with correction, left eye
fundus photography / dilated fundus exam findings
screening.value: scattered microaneurysms, dot-blot hemorrhages, and hard exudates; no retinal tear or retinal detachment

conditions.name: moderate nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy, right eye
severe nonproliferative diabetic retinopathy, left eye
center-involving diabetic macular edema, left eye
type 2 diabetes mellitus with chronic hyperglycemia

medications.name: bevacizumab 1.25 mg/0.05 mL intravitreal injection
medications.sig: Inject 1.25 mg/0.05 mL intravitreally into the left eye once on 2025-03-06.
medications.reason: center-involving diabetic macular edema of the left eye

procedures.name: intravitreal injection of bevacizumab into the left eye
procedures.indication: center-involving diabetic macular edema in the left eye with reduced visual acuity and OCT-confirmed increased central subfield thickness
procedures.technique_details: Topical anesthesia, povidone-iodine prep, intravitreal injection of bevacizumab 1.25 mg/0.05 mL in the left eye, post-injection precautions reviewed.

document_references.name: Retina consultation note
document_references.description: Summarizes referral after abnormal diabetic eye screening, blurred central vision in the left eye, intake vitals and morning glucose history, bilateral retinal examination findings, OCT interpretation, assessment of asymmetric diabetic retinopathy severity, counseling about glycemic and blood-pressure control, and plan for follow-up in 4 to 6 weeks.

document_references.name: OCT macula report
document_references.description: Summarizes macular OCT findings for both eyes, including right-eye central macular thickness 289 microns without center-involving edema and left-eye center-involving diabetic macular edema with central subfield thickness 412 microns supporting treatment.

document_references.name: Fundus imaging report
document_references.description: Summarizes fundus photography and dilated examination findings, including scattered microaneurysms, dot-blot hemorrhages, hard exudates, asymmetric diabetic retinopathy severity, and absence of retinal tear or detachment.

document_references.name: Procedure note
document_references.description: Documents the left-eye intravitreal bevacizumab injection performed by Dr. Kapur, including indication, medication and dose, topical anesthesia, povidone-iodine prep, support by Teresa Lucero, RN, and immediate tolerance / post-procedure precautions.

document_references.name: Post-injection instructions
document_references.description: Summarizes return precautions after intravitreal injection, including worsening pain, vision loss, increasing redness, flashes, floaters, or curtain-like vision change, along with the planned retina follow-up window of 4 to 6 weeks.

diagnostic_reports.type: optical coherence tomography of the macula, both eyes
diagnostic_reports.description: Imaging report documents right-eye central macular thickness of 289 microns without center-involving edema and left-eye center-involving diabetic macular edema with central subfield thickness 412 microns; used to guide same-day treatment decision.
Imaging report documents scattered microaneurysms, dot-blot hemorrhages, hard exudates, bilateral diabetic retinopathy with greater severity in the left eye, and no retinal tear or detachment.
